summary: Preserve each field's parsed scalar/container kind (string/integer/number/boolean/list/map/null) in the projection — the precondition a sound `type` check needs.

per.section: Decision: the `type` vocabulary is a closed scalar/container lattice

edit.description: Stop collapsing every scalar to a bare string for kind purposes: extend the projection so each field records its source kind off the parsed YAML/JSON value (`json_scalar_string` currently flattens bool/number/string alike). Keep `FeatureValue::as_scalar` yielding the comparison text the scalar predicates (`min_len`/`max_len`/`enum`/`deny`/`allowed_chars`) read unchanged. Tests: an integer, boolean, string, and list field each project with their source kind preserved while `as_scalar` still returns the field text.

Update `FeatureValue` construction sites in the engine's unit-test helpers if the variant shape changes (the scalar-text path in `scalar()` is unchanged — every existing scalar predicate keeps reading text). Edit only as needed to keep the engine tests green.

tests.asserts: a field's source kind is preserved in the projection; as_scalar still yields the field text for the existing scalar predicates

acceptance: the projection records each field's source kind (string/integer/number/boolean/list/map/null); scalar predicates still read field text unchanged; existing extract + engine tests pass.
notes: The spec names this exact correction: stringifying every scalar (the slice-1 shortcut, extract.rs) makes `type` undecidable and is corrected before the primitive ships. Foundation only — no new predicate here; TYPE-PRIMITIVE adds the clause. FeatureValue's enum shape may change (Scalar(String) carrying a kind), which ripples to engine.rs test construction — hence engine.rs is in the blast radius.

summary: Add the `type` field primitive over the closed scalar/container lattice — `Predicate::Type`, parsed and decided against the preserved source kind.

per.section: Decision: the `type` vocabulary is a closed scalar/container lattice

edit.description: Add `Predicate::Type { field, kind }` to the closed vocabulary; parse the `type` clause (the declared type must be one of the closed lattice string/integer/number/boolean/list/map/null — an unknown type is a load error, mirroring UnknownPredicate); add the `type` arm to `predicate_key`. Tests: a `type` clause parses; an unknown declared type is a load error.

Add the `Predicate::Type` arm to `decide`: compare the field's preserved source kind (from TYPED-EXTRACTION) to the declared kind; an absent field is the `required` clause's concern (Holds), so `type` stays silent on absence. Tests: fires on a kind mismatch, silent on a match and on an absent field.

tests.asserts: a `type` clause parses into the closed lattice and rejects an unknown declared type at load
`type` fires exactly when a field's source kind differs from the declared kind, silent on match/absence

acceptance: a `type` clause parses over the closed lattice, rejects an unknown type at load, and fires exactly when a field's preserved source kind differs from the declared kind.
notes: Closed lattice only (string/integer/number/boolean/list/map/null) — no formats, unions, nested schemas, or numeric ranges; those are the JSON-Schema unsound-proxy surface the bespoke-closed-algebra Decision rejects. `min_len`/`max_len`/`enum`/`pattern` refine within a scalar type; `type` only fixes the kind.
